title: ProgressDialog Object
description: Provides access to the progress dialog.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: hide
methods.description: Hides the progress dialog. This should be used when the process has completed.

methods.name: reset
methods.description: Method that resets the progress bar. The progress bar "rewinds" and shows no progress. This is the same as setting the progress value to the minimum value.

methods.name: show
methods.description: Displays the progress dialog that includes a progress bar that can be used to display a continually updated message indicating the progress of a process that will take more than a few seconds. The progress is determined by comparing the current progress value with the minimum and maximum values.

properties.name: cancelButtonText
properties.description: Sets the text label on the Cancel button. The default text label is "Cancel".

properties.name: isBackgroundTranslucent
properties.description: Gets and sets if the dialog background is translucent. This is false by default

properties.name: isCancelButtonShown
properties.description: Gets and sets if the cancel button is included in the dialog. This is false by default.

properties.name: isShowing
properties.description: Gets if the Progress Dialog is currently being displayed

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: maximumValue
properties.description: The maximum value of the progress bar. This is used along with the minimum value and the progress value to compute the current percentage complete.

properties.name: message
properties.description: Gets and sets the message to display along with the progress bar. The following symbols can be used to display values. "%p" is replaced by the percentage completed. "%v" is replaced by the current value. %m is replaced by the total number of steps. For example, the message "Processing section %v of %m." will result in the message "Processing section 1 of 10." to be displayed if the maximum value is 10 and the current value is 1. Specify an empty string ("") for no message to appear along with the progress panel.

properties.name: minimumValue
properties.description: The minimum value of the progress bar. This is used along with the maximum value and the progress value to compute the current percentage complete. This is also the initial progress value when the progress bar is first displayed.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: progressValue
properties.description: Gets and sets the current progress bar value. Progress is determined based on this value relative to the minimum and maximum values. This will update the values displayed in the message string.

properties.name: title
properties.description: Gets and sets the title of the progress dialog

properties.name: wasCancelled
properties.description: Indicates if the cancel button was selected the last time the Progress Dialog was shown.

related_samples.name: Generate Toolpaths API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates generating the toolpaths in the active document.

related_samples.name: Progress Dialog API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates how to use progress dialog

cancelButtonText.title: ProgressDialog.cancelButtonText Property
cancelButtonText.description: Sets the text label on the Cancel button. The default text label is "Cancel".

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = progressDialog_var.cancelButtonText

# Set the value of the property.
progressDialog_var.cancelButtonText = propertyValue

classType.title: ProgressDialog.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

hide.title: ProgressDialog.hide Method
hide.description: Hides the progress dialog. This should be used when the process has completed.

related_samples.name: Progress Dialog API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates how to use progress dialog

reset.title: ProgressDialog.reset Method
reset.description: Method that resets the progress bar. The progress bar "rewinds" and shows no progress. This is the same as setting the progress value to the minimum value.

show.title: ProgressDialog.show Method
show.description: Displays the progress dialog that includes a progress bar that can be used to display a continually updated message indicating the progress of a process that will take more than a few seconds. The progress is determined by comparing the current progress value with the minimum and maximum values.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = progressDialog_var.show(title, message, minimumValue, maximumValue)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = progressDialog_var.show(title, message, minimumValue, maximumValue, delay)

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Sets the title for the progress dialog

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: The message to display along with the progress bar. The following symbols can be used to display values. "%p" is replaced by the percentage completed. "%v" is replaced by the current value. %m is replaced by the total number of steps. For example, the message "Processing section %v of %m." will result in the message "Processing section 1 of 10." to be displayed if the maximum value is 10 and the current value is 1.

parameters.name: minimumValue
parameters.description: The minimum value of the progress bar. This is used along with the maximum value and the progress value to compute the current percentage complete. This is also the initial progress value when the progress bar is first displayed.

parameters.name: maximumValue
parameters.description: The maximum value of the progress bar. This is used along with the minimum value and the progress value to compute the current percentage complete.

parameters.name: delay
parameters.description: Specifies the time interval in seconds to delay displaying the Progress Dialog. This provides a way to hide the progress dialog before it actually gets displayed, which is useful for cases where the progress of the operation being tracked completes quickly and there is no need to indicate progress to the user.   This is an optional argument whose default value is 0.

related_samples.name: Generate Toolpaths API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates generating the toolpaths in the active document.

related_samples.name: Progress Dialog API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates how to use progress dialog
